item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Permission to test SCP-1225 with bottles of store-bought alcoholic drinks will not be granted.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I think this is made nicely so good job ill give you that +1

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired, edits made.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block 
- hide block

While reading this article, the people in 784 came to mind since they are so cheerful due to 784's affects (the 'Christmas Spirit' in particular), so I thought that perhaps they wouldn't be as severely effected by 1225's affects, almost as if the two would cancel each other out. That being said, recommending the following addition to the second addenda:

The acquisition of D-Class personnel from SCP-784 is currently under consideration due to the fact that these personnel are less prone to equipment failures caused by SCP-1225. Further research is required in order to determine the exact nature of this effect. Containment protocols have been updated.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Coming back to this, since Christmas is getting closer.

This brings me horrible and depressing images in my head, a kid running in excitement to the wonderful presents of a christmas tree, only for disappointment and sadness to pop in his face.

Maybe it was because I was spoiled, but this is some sad stuff and I love it. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Protected from the weather, while not being enclosed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"on a concrete pedestal that is protected from the elements"
That sounds weird… Do you mean weather proof or is immune to the "elements" of scp-1225

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Army veteran comes home for christmas and hides in box D: its probably too slow for that but still upvoted for hilarity and possible creepiness

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose this being SCP 12/25 is intentional. So the group has a sense of humor, I see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Individually, everything is fine. They don't really hang together properly, though—if you have to explain it in the discussion, then you've got a problem with the writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First addendum could be taken out, adding a secondary effect as an afterthought just makes this kind of confusing.

I really liked the idea of someone hanging this from their Christmas tree and it turning all the holiday visitors into malnourished, abused, old people.

+1

item.title: Re: Worst Christmas Ever?
item.content:encoded: Well, if the trigger mechanism of the BB gun malfunctioned, then it could shatter, sending small shrapnel backwards to strike the shooter in the eye. So yes, someone could shoot their eye out. :-P

item.title: Worst Christmas Ever?
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't someone be shooting their own eye out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here's your Gameboy! It even came with Pokemon Yellow! Too bad all the 'mon are missingno and you can't actually get out of the first town…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And no one ended up having to wear pink bunny pajamas, so no, not the worst Christmas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just found this because of the name and it is awesome

but I WANTED A GAMEBOY!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, this feels like there's a reference I'm missing here. Why decay presents? Why Christmas trees? I dunno… it's not interesting to me as it stands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a Christmas tree ornament that's the personification of bad Christmases? Huh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the primary effect, and it leaves room for lots of imagination. Don't like the first addendum at all, though… I think it takes away more than it adds. Like the second one, though… could be a little less wordy, but I like "update" style addendum, if they make sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate that the containment protocols have been updated. That kind of continuing story is what keeps this site interesting to me. And the SCP's effects are interesting as well. +1.

item.title: Ehh...
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't particularly appeal to me, but I approve in that not all SCPs -need- to be giant constructs with impressive effects and all that. The effects are rather boring and standard, but fairly well written.

My opinion is neutral, so I'll count this as a neutral vote. Well done either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Getting crappy gifts makes for a bad Christmas, but doesn't make for the worst Christmas. That requires vicious drunken brawls that end in tears, recriminations and hospital visits. Possibly a permanent trip to jail, too.

Thanks for the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice, although I don't think it needs the secondary effect. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bet the Grinches did this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. Nice retro charm, without being boring.

+1
